question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user open a large number of concurrent gRPC subscriptions via listen() during a Redis network partition, causing the retry backoff loop in RedisTopicListener to accumulate unbounded thread/connection resources on the boundedElastic scheduler, leading to server-side OOM or thread starvation that denies service to all other subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getTopicListener()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getTopicListener() selects the listener type at call time from listenerProperties without any per-request locking, so that during a network partition where the operator switches type from REDIS to POLL mid-stream, the user's active Flux silently switches data sources and receives messages from a different backend without notification, causing data integrity violations?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: filterMessage(TopicMessage, TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user, during a Redis network partition and subsequent recovery, receive duplicate topic messages that pass filterMessage() because the consensusTimestamp >= filter.getStartTime() check does not deduplicate replayed messages re-published to Redis after reconnection, leading to message replay attacks against the subscriber?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: recordMetric(TopicMessage)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a subscription that triggers recordMetric() with a consensusTimestamp value so large that the division consensusTimestamp / 1000000 overflows or produces a value greater than System.currentTimeMillis(), causing latency to be negative and silently dropped (latency > 0 check), permanently corrupting the hiero.mirror.grpc.consensus.latency metric and blinding operators to real latency during partition recovery?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a TopicMessageFilter with startTime set to Long.MIN_VALUE (minimum long), causing filterMessage() to pass every message in the system during partition recovery when the shared poll listener replays all buffered messages, resulting in mass unauthorized data disclosure across all topic IDs?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: filterMessage(TopicMessage, TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the filterMessage() check message.getTopicId().equals(filter.getTopicId()) during a network partition where SharedPollingTopicListener broadcasts all topic messages on a single shared Flux, by subscribing to a high-volume topic to infer the existence and message rate of other topics whose messages are filtered client-side but still processed server-side, constituting a side-channel information leak?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user hold open a gRPC stream via listen() indefinitely during a database network partition, causing PollingTopicListener to enter an infinite retry loop with Long.MAX_VALUE repetitions that never terminates, exhausting database connection pool resources and preventing legitimate users from obtaining connections after partition heals?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getTopicListener()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the UnsupportedOperationException thrown by getTopicListener() default case by racing a configuration reload during a network partition event, causing an unhandled exception that propagates through the reactive Flux and terminates all active subscriber streams simultaneously, resulting in a denial of service?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that listen() returns Flux.empty() when listenerProperties.isEnabled() is false, and that a network partition may cause the enabled flag to toggle, to detect internal infrastructure state changes (partition detection) by observing when their subscription stream terminates with empty vs. error, constituting an information disclosure oracle?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: recordMetric(TopicMessage)] Can an unprivileged external user flood the system with subscriptions during a network partition recovery, causing recordMetric() to be called at extremely high frequency and saturate the MeterRegistry timer hiero.mirror.grpc.consensus.latency with millions of samples, leading to metric system memory exhaustion and denial of service for monitoring infrastructure?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: filterMessage(TopicMessage, TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the consensusTimestamp >= filter.getStartTime() comparison in filterMessage() during partition recovery, where the database replays messages with artificially old timestamps, to receive messages that should have been filtered out by the startTime boundary, causing unauthorized access to historical messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user open thousands of subscriptions to the same topicId during a Redis network partition, causing RedisTopicListener.topicMessages ConcurrentHashMap to be populated with a single shared Flux that is then cancelled when any one subscriber disconnects (via doOnCancel -> unsubscribe), terminating all other subscribers' streams simultaneously and causing a denial of service?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getTopicListener()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of fallback logic in getTopicListener() during a network partition, where the REDIS listener is unavailable but no automatic failover to POLL occurs, causing all new listen() calls to silently fail or block indefinitely rather than falling back to a working listener, resulting in complete message delivery failure?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the reactive chain in listen() where filter() and doOnNext() are applied after getTopicListener().listen(filter), so that during a partition recovery burst of messages, the downstream filter pressure causes backpressure overflow in the maxBufferSize (16384) buffer, dropping messages silently and causing the subscriber to miss critical topic messages without any error signal?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: recordMetric(TopicMessage)] Can an unprivileged external user cause recordMetric() to record a latency of Long.MAX_VALUE milliseconds by subscribing during a network partition where consensusTimestamp is 0 or near-zero (uninitialized), causing System.currentTimeMillis() - 0 to produce an astronomically large latency value that corrupts percentile histograms in the MeterRegistry permanently?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: filterMessage(TopicMessage, TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that filterMessage() performs no null check on message.getTopicId() before calling .equals(), so that during a network partition where a malformed or partially-deserialized TopicMessage arrives with a null topicId, a NullPointerException terminates the entire shared Flux and disconnects all subscribers on that topic?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lazy initialization of the consensusLatencyTimer (Getter lazy=true) in a high-concurrency partition recovery scenario, where multiple threads simultaneously call recordMetric() for the first time, causing a race condition in the lazy getter that results in multiple Timer instances being registered with the MeterRegistry, leading to metric duplication or IllegalArgumentException crashes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the network partition scenario where the Redis connection is lost and the retryWhen(Retry.backoff(Long.MAX_VALUE, interval)) in RedisTopicListener never gives up, by holding a subscription open indefinitely and preventing the container Mono.cache() from being invalidated, so that after partition heals the stale cached container is reused and messages are never received?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: filterMessage(TopicMessage, TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a TopicMessageFilter with endTime set to a value just after startTime but far in the past, and during a network partition where the system clock skews, cause filterMessage()'s consensusTimestamp >= startTime check to pass for messages that should be outside the requested window, receiving messages they are not entitled to?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of rate limiting in listen() to open a flood of subscriptions during a network partition event, each triggering a new retry backoff chain in the underlying listener, causing exponential growth in scheduled tasks on the Schedulers.boundedElastic() pool and eventually exhausting the scheduler's thread limit, denying service to all users?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getTopicListener()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getTopicListener() is called on every listen() invocation and reads listenerProperties.getType() without synchronization, so that during a network partition where the type is being changed concurrently, the user's subscription receives a partially-initialized listener that delivers messages from an inconsistent state?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the SharedPollingTopicListener's single shared Flux (topicMessages) during a network partition, where the shared Flux is cancelled due to all subscribers disconnecting, causing the next subscriber to trigger a new poll starting from DomainUtils.now() (onStart), thereby skipping all messages produced during the partition window and causing permanent message loss for that subscriber?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: recordMetric(TopicMessage)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a network partition that causes the MeterRegistry to become unavailable, and then flood the system with messages that call recordMetric(), causing getConsensusLatencyTimer() to throw an exception that propagates through the doOnNext() reactive operator and terminates the subscriber's Flux with an error, constituting a denial of service?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: filterMessage(TopicMessage, TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the filterMessage() logic during a partition recovery where the SharedPollingTopicListener replays messages from lastConsensusTimestamp, by subscribing with a startTime equal to 0, causing all replayed messages to pass the consensusTimestamp >= 0 check and receiving the entire message history buffered during the partition?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that listen() applies filterMessage() as a client-side filter after the underlying listener delivers messages, so that during a network partition where SharedPollingTopicListener broadcasts all topics to all subscribers, the CPU cost of filtering millions of irrelevant messages per subscriber causes CPU exhaustion and denial of service for the gRPC server?
